comments.author: reader
comments.text: That's fair. Because it immediately benefits me to go the browser for anything over 200 words (or 15 lines, actually), you should really have the social fear of writing dense info to a console interface that I hate.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: There's also the cost that you waste tokens on putting too much **thought** into a question where I just wanted a gut answer to iterate on. That cost gets paid whether you do it via console or browser; I just feel it more severely in the console. You can always push back when you don't know the scope of WHAT I am asking you to produce, regardless of the medium.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: Let's make a dedicated directory for this. For things where it's PURELY conversational ergonomics, let's just use a sequence number: random555.htm then random556.htm, etc.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: Yeah, I don't really need terseness once I'm in the browser. It's actually good at times to let you expand a bit.
